# redpanda_secret (Resource)

Defines the secret resource.

Creates a secret in a Redpanda cluster's per-cluster secret store. Other resources reference the value via `${secrets.<NAME>}` (e.g. shadow link SCRAM passwords).

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

> **NOTE**: [Write-only arguments](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments) are supported in Terraform 1.11 and later.

- `cluster_api_url` (String) Dataplane API URL of the cluster that owns this secret (`redpanda_cluster.<name>.cluster_api_url`). Immutable; changing this prevents deletion of the existing secret. Generally easier to recreate the resource than to change this.
- `name` (String) Secret identifier.
- `scopes` (List of String) Secret scopes
- `secret_data` (String, Sensitive, [Write-only](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/resources/ephemeral#write-only-arguments)) The secret data. Must be Base64-encoded.

### Optional

- `allow_deletion` (Boolean) Allows deletion of the secret. Defaults to false.
- `labels` (Map of String) Secret labels.
- `secret_data_version` (Number) Version counter for `secret_data`. Increment this each time you change `secret_data`; Terraform compares versions to decide when to call UpdateSecret (write-only values can't be diffed directly). TF-only construct â€” not part of the API.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Secret identifier.

## Example Usage

```terraform
provider "redpanda" {}

variable "secret_value" {
  type        = string
  sensitive   = true
  description = "Value to store in the secret"
}

resource "redpanda_resource_group" "example" {
  name = "example-resource-group"
}

resource "redpanda_network" "example" {
  name              = "example-network"
  resource_group_id = redpanda_resource_group.example.id
  cloud_provider    = "aws"
  region            = "us-west-2"
  cluster_type      = "dedicated"
  cidr_block        = "10.0.0.0/20"
}

resource "redpanda_cluster" "example" {
  name              = "example-cluster"
  resource_group_id = redpanda_resource_group.example.id
  network_id        = redpanda_network.example.id
  cloud_provider    = "aws"
  region            = "us-west-2"
  cluster_type      = "dedicated"
  connection_type   = "public"
  throughput_tier   = "tier-1-aws"
  zones             = ["us-west-2a", "us-west-2b", "us-west-2c"]
}

resource "redpanda_secret" "example" {
  name                = "EXAMPLE_SECRET"
  secret_data         = var.secret_value
  secret_data_version = 1
  scopes              = ["SCOPE_REDPANDA_CLUSTER"]
  labels              = { team = "data" }
  cluster_api_url     = redpanda_cluster.example.cluster_api_url
  allow_deletion      = true
}
```

## Security Considerations

`secret_data` is a write-only attribute (Terraform 1.11+). The plaintext is never written to Terraform state. To rotate the value, change `secret_data` and increment `secret_data_version` â€” the version counter is what Terraform diffs against, since write-only values cannot be compared directly.

## Scopes

`scopes` declares which features may consume the secret. The dataplane API enforces scope-based access â€” supplying a scope that doesn't match the consumer (e.g. setting only `SCOPE_REDPANDA_CONNECT` and trying to reference the secret from a `redpanda_shadow_link`) will cause the consumer to fail at runtime. Multiple scopes can be combined.

| Scope | Used by |
|---|---|
| `SCOPE_REDPANDA_CLUSTER` | `redpanda_shadow_link` (SCRAM/PLAIN passwords, TLS keys) |
| `SCOPE_REDPANDA_CONNECT` | `redpanda_pipeline` |
| `SCOPE_MCP_SERVER` | MCP server resources |
| `SCOPE_AI_AGENT` | AI agent resources |
| `SCOPE_AI_GATEWAY` | AI gateway resources |

## Labels

The dataplane API auto-injects a managed `owner` label (e.g. `owner=console`). User-supplied labels are merged in at create time and round-trip through state. Per the API, labels are immutable on Update â€” to change them you must destroy and recreate the secret.

## Import

```shell
terraform import redpanda_secret.example secretName,clusterId
```

Where `secretName` is the secret's identifier (uppercase, matching `^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$`) and `clusterId` is the ID of the cluster in Redpanda Cloud. After import, `secret_data` is unset until the next apply provides a value.

## API Reference

For more information, see the [Redpanda Cloud Data Plane API â€“ Secrets](https://docs.redpanda.com/api/doc/cloud-dataplane/tag/Secrets/) documentation.
